-{"id":"bd-1ub","title":"Close Rich port gaps: parity, conformance, missing features","description":"Goal: close every remaining gap in the Python Rich -> rich_rust port by (1) establishing authoritative parity documentation, (2) adding true conformance tests against Python Rich output, and (3) implementing currently-missing features (Live, Layout, logging handler, HTML/SVG export) or explicitly documenting scope decisions.\n\nWhy: README still lists missing Rich features; spec/docs are out of sync with implemented functionality; conformance tests do not compare against Python Rich. This epic coordinates the remediation so future work does not rely on tribal knowledge.\n\nDefinition of done: FEATURE_PARITY.md exists and is authoritative; RICH_SPEC/README are consistent; conformance fixtures exist and are enforced; each missing feature is either implemented with tests/docs or explicitly excluded with rationale.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:24:50.961255334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T00:24:50.961255334Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Include: component list, status (implemented / partial / missing / out-of-scope), feature flags, relevant modules, tests/fixtures coverage, and links to spec sections.\n\nRationale: We need a single source of truth so future work does not rely on README snapshots or memory.\n\nAcceptance: Document exists, covers all core and optional features, and clearly distinguishes planned vs out-of-scope vs implemented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:25:03.931967911Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T00:37:32.226055138Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T00:37:32.225867244Z","close_reason":"FEATURE_PARITY.md created and populated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:25:03.931967911Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:33:42.822208218Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:34:12.674929982Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Keep spec sections consistent with current modules and feature flags.\n\nAcceptance: RICH_SPEC.md accurately reflects current implementation and planned gaps.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:25:09.897648740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T00:37:13.563589869Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T00:37:13.563342954Z","close_reason":"Updated exclusions to match implementation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:25:09.897648740Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ub.1.3","title":"Update README feature parity section to match spec + parity doc","description":"Update README and FEATURE_PARITY.md to accurately reflect implementation status.

**CRITICAL FINDING:** FEATURE_PARITY.md is outdated. It says:
- Live: 'Missing (planned)' - BUT src/live.rs exists (~15KB implementation)
- Layout: 'Missing (planned)' - BUT src/renderables/layout.rs exists (~485 lines)

**Tasks:**
1. Update FEATURE_PARITY.md:
   - Change Live status from 'Missing (planned)' to 'Partial' (impl exists, needs tests)
   - Change Layout status from 'Missing (planned)' to 'Partial' (impl exists, needs tests)
   - Add notes about what's implemented vs what needs testing

2. Update README Feature Parity section:
   - Align with corrected FEATURE_PARITY.md
   - Clearly separate: Implemented, Partial (needs tests), Missing (planned), Out-of-scope
   - Add link to FEATURE_PARITY.md for authoritative status

3. Verify all documentation is consistent:
   - FEATURE_PARITY.md
   - README.md
   - RICH_SPEC.md references

**Acceptance:**
- FEATURE_PARITY.md accurately reflects Live/Layout as 'Partial'
- README no longer contradicts implementation
- All three docs are consistent","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:25:18.571132077Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T03:20:36.426915709Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T03:20:36.423354570Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:25:18.571132077Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:33:49.869904979Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:33:52.995986126Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:34:16.092385545Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -26,7 +26,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1ub.3.13","title":"Live impl: stdout/stderr redirection + render hooks","description":"Implement stdout/stderr redirection and render hooks for Live.

**Reference documents:**
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 16 - Python Rich Live behavior spec
- PROPOSED_RUST_RICH_ARCHITECTURE.md Section 6 - Rust Live architecture design

**Implementation scope:**
1. Implement stdout/stderr redirection:
   - When redirect_stdout=true, capture stdout writes
   - When redirect_stderr=true, capture stderr writes
   - Buffer captured output
   - Print captured output before Live refresh
   - Restore original streams on stop/Drop

2. Implement render hooks:
   - Console::begin_live() hook
   - Console::end_live() hook
   - Allow custom pre/post render callbacks

3. Implement context manager pattern:
   - Live::scope() or enter()/exit() pattern
   - RAII-style cleanup
   - Panic-safe (cleanup on unwind)

4. Handle edge cases:
   - Nested Live contexts (error or queue)
   - Exception/panic during render
   - Signal interrupts (SIGINT, SIGTERM)
   - Screen resize events (SIGWINCH)

**Implementation notes:**
- Use std::io::set_output_capture or similar
- Consider crossterm or termion for terminal control
- Ensure thread-safety with Arc/Mutex

**Acceptance:**
- stdout/stderr redirection works correctly
- Context manager pattern is ergonomic
- No resource leaks
- Panic-safe cleanup","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:44:05.750160654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T03:34:30.094480852Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T03:34:30.094115273Z","close_reason":"Completed (best-effort proxy writers; no global stdio redirection under no-unsafe)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.13","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:44:05.750160654Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.13","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:45:22.672871130Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.13","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:30:21.891040352Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.13","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:45:18.568960436Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ub.3.14","title":"Live tests: unit + conformance fixtures","description":"Add comprehensive unit tests for Live functionality:

**Core lifecycle tests:**
- Live::new() initialization with various options
- start/stop state transitions
- refresh() behavior (manual and auto)
- update() renderable replacement
- Drop safety (cursor restore, alt-screen cleanup)

**Terminal control tests:**
- Cursor hide/show emission
- Alt-screen enter/exit
- Clear line / clear screen codes
- Scroll region handling

**Integration tests:**
- Console integration points
- Stdout/stderr redirection verification
- Transient vs persistent render modes
- Vertical overflow handling

**Conformance fixtures:**
- Capture Python Rich Live output for key scenarios
- Rust output comparison tests
- Visual regression fixtures if feasible

Each test should include:
- Clear test name describing the behavior
- Arrange/Act/Assert structure
- Edge case coverage (non-TTY, zero-width, etc.)

Acceptance: >90% code coverage for Live module, all edge cases documented, conformance fixtures in place.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:44:11.592359857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T03:54:03.352392117Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T03:54:03.352276549Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive Live tests in tests/conformance/live_tests.rs with 42 test cases covering: initialization (new, with_options, invalid refresh rate), lifecycle (start/stop cycles, idempotency, drop safety), refresh (outputs content, callbacks), update (changes content), vertical overflow (crop, ellipsis, visible), proxy writers (stdout, stderr, LiveWriter), terminal control (cursor hide/show), transient vs persistent modes, auto-refresh thread, edge cases (empty, unicode, rapid updates). All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:44:11.592359857Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:45:28.521166657Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:45:32.749678870Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:45:38.617499458Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ub.3.15","title":"Live docs/examples","description":"Write user-facing docs for Live usage, options, and caveats. Add an example (e.g., examples/live.rs) and update README + FEATURE_PARITY as needed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:44:18.727731427Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T03:34:44.647455211Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T03:34:44.647160777Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:44:18.727731427Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:45:43.495729388Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:45:48.955094802Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:45:54.866777687Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1ub.3.16","title":"Live e2e test script with detailed logging","description":"Create an executable e2e test script (examples/e2e_live.rs or tests/e2e_live.rs) that exercises the complete Live workflow:
- Start/stop/refresh cycles
- Transient vs persistent rendering
- Auto-refresh with various intervals
- Context manager (scoped) usage
- Stdout/stderr redirection verification
- Terminal control code emission (cursor hide/show, alt-screen)
- Nested Live handling
- Error recovery and cleanup

The script should use structured logging (tracing or env_logger) at DEBUG level to output:
- Timestamps for each operation
- State transitions (Idle -> Running -> Stopped)
- Render frame counts and timing
- Control code sequences being emitted
- Any errors or warnings encountered

Acceptance: Script runs standalone, produces detailed log output, and exits 0 on success. Can be run in CI with RUST_LOG=debug.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:01:25.067326635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T02:02:12.359345908Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:01:25.067326635Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:02:12.359268983Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:02:11.478802466Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.3.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub.3.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:02:10.653183175Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -42,7 +42,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1ub.4.12","title":"Layout impl: render + measure integration","description":"Implement Layout rendering and measurement integration.

**Reference documents:**
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 17 - Python Rich Layout behavior spec
- PROPOSED_RUST_RICH_ARCHITECTURE.md Section 7 - Rust Layout architecture design

**Implementation scope:**
1. Implement sizing algorithm:
   - allocate_space(available: usize, layouts: &[Layout]) -> Vec<usize>
   - Honor fixed size constraints first
   - Distribute remaining space by ratio
   - Respect minimum_size constraints
   - Handle edge case: more fixed sizes than available

2. Implement Region struct:
   - x, y, width, height coordinates
   - Track each layout's allocated region

3. Implement measure():
   - Calculate minimum and preferred dimensions
   - Recursively measure children
   - Account for splitter overhead

4. Implement render():
   - Allocate regions based on available space
   - Render each child into its region
   - Render splitters between children
   - Handle visibility (skip hidden layouts)
   - Compose final output

5. Implement ConsoleRender trait:
   - render(&self, console, options) -> Vec<Segment>
   - measure(&self, console, max_width) -> Measurement

**Key algorithms:**
- Ratio distribution: size[i] = (ratio[i] / sum_ratios) * available
- Fixed allocation: deduct fixed sizes first, then distribute
- Minimum enforcement: never go below minimum_size

**Acceptance:**
- Sizing algorithm produces correct allocations
- Nested layouts render correctly
- Splitters appear between children
- Works with various console widths

 {"id":"bd-1ub.4.13","title":"Layout tests + conformance fixtures","description":"Add comprehensive unit tests for Layout functionality:

**Tree structure tests:**
- split_row/split_column operations
- Nested split creation (3+ levels)
- Named region lookup and update
- Visibility toggling
- Tree traversal and iteration

**Sizing algorithm tests:**
- Ratio distribution (various ratios)
- Minimum size enforcement
- Fixed size allocation
- Available space distribution
- Edge cases: zero width, single child, all hidden

**Rendering tests:**
- Region boundary calculation
- Content placement within regions
- Splitter rendering (row/column separators)
- Console options propagation

**Conformance fixtures:**
- Capture Python Rich Layout output
- Rust output comparison
- Visual regression for complex layouts

Each test should include:
- Clear test name with layout configuration
- Explicit expected dimensions/positions
- Boundary condition coverage

Acceptance: >90% code coverage for Layout module, sizing algorithm fully tested, conformance fixtures in place.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:46:49.810336448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T03:50:16.109174819Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T03:50:16.109155783Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive layout tests in tests/conformance/layout_tests.rs with 36 test cases covering: tree structure (split_row, split_column, nested 3-4 levels, named lookup, visibility, unsplit/add_split), sizing algorithm (ratios, fixed size, minimum size, mixed sizing, edge cases), rendering (correct dimensions, content placement), Region/LayoutSplitter structs, and edge cases (zero width, very narrow/short, empty children).  {"id":"bd-2llx","title":"Regression test: Cell::new() vs Cell::from_markup()","description":"## Problem
Cell::new() was silently NOT parsing markup - users expected Cell::new("[bold]text[/]") to work but it didn't. We added Cell::from_markup() but need regression tests to prevent this from happening again and document the difference clearly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test that explicitly shows Cell::new("[bold]text[/]") does NOT parse markup (expected behavior, documents it)
- [ ] Test that explicitly shows Cell::from_markup("[bold]text[/]") DOES parse markup
- [ ] Test the difference in output between the two methods
- [ ] Add doc comments explaining when to use each method

## Implementation Notes
- The tests should be clear about the EXPECTED difference between the two methods
- This documents API behavior and prevents confusion

## Files to modify
- src/renderables/table.rs - improve doc comments
- tests/conformance/table_tests.rs - add regression tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:27.981429151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:25:04.610863384Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:25:04.610835742Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 6 regression tests documenting Cell::new() vs Cell::from_markup() difference. Updated Cell::new() doc comments with clear examples. All tests pass, clippy and fmt clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2nv","title":"Targeted code review pass + file findings","description":"Perform focused code review for correctness/perf risks; file follow-up beads for any findings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T16:59:53.973055499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:21:57.601872493Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:21:57.601543252Z","close_reason":"Completed: filed findings bd-yb9 + bd-1wk","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2rqt","title":"API misuse detection tests","description":"## Problem
Users can easily misuse the API (e.g., using Cell::new() instead of Cell::from_markup()). We need tests that document these gotchas and ensure the API behaves predictably.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Document all methods that accept strings but DON'T parse markup
- [ ] Document all methods that DO parse markup  
- [ ] Add tests that explicitly show the difference
- [ ] Consider adding deprecation warnings or lints for confusing APIs

## API Audit checklist
- [ ] Cell::new() - does NOT parse markup (documented)
- [ ] Cell::from_markup() - DOES parse markup (documented)
- [ ] Text::new() - does NOT parse markup
- [ ] Text::from_markup() - DOES parse markup
- [ ] Panel title/content - audit and document
- [ ] Tree labels - audit and document
- [ ] Progress descriptions - audit and document

## Files to modify
- Add doc comments to all relevant structs/methods
- tests/conformance/*.rs - add explicit documentation tests","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:09.661046714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:09.661046714Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}

 {"id":"bd-2thu","title":"E2E tests: markup parsing across all renderables","description":"## Problem
The table cell markup parsing bug (where [bold #2ecc71]PLAY[/] showed literally instead of being styled) was not caught by existing tests. This indicates a gap in our testing strategy - we need E2E tests that verify markup is actually parsed and rendered correctly across ALL renderables.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create E2E tests that verify markup tags are NOT present in final rendered output
- [ ] Test markup parsing in: Table cells, Panel titles/content, Text, Tree nodes, Progress bar descriptions
- [ ] Each test should render to a string buffer and assert markup tags like [bold], [red], etc. are NOT in output
- [ ] Tests should verify styles ARE applied (check for ANSI codes or styled segments)

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Pattern: render content with markup -> assert raw markup tags absent -> assert styling present
- These are integration tests, not unit tests

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/table_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests  
- tests/conformance/text_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- May need to create new test modules","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:26.168768880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690653345Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690621495Z","close_reason":"Added E2E markup parsing tests across renderables; validated e2e_rendering","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
-{"id":"bd-3svf","title":"Full rendering pipeline integration tests","description":"## Problem
We need integration tests that verify the ENTIRE rendering pipeline from user API call to final output string. This would have caught the Cell markup bug.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Console.print() with various renderables containing markup
- [ ] Capture actual output string and verify markup is processed
- [ ] Test Table -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test Panel -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test nested renderables (Table in Panel, etc.) with markup

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Check that no raw markup tags like [bold] remain in output
- Verify ANSI escape codes are present for styled content
- These are TRUE integration tests - testing the full stack

## Files to modify
- Create new tests/integration/*.rs if needed
- Or add to tests/conformance/ with clear naming","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:07.703397468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:25:30.755931995Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}

 {"id":"bd-4zjp","title":"Audit existing tests for trivial assertions","description":"## Problem
We found trivial assertions like assert!(x.is_empty() || true) and assert!(!output.is_empty() || output.is_empty()) that were always true and provided no value. There may be more lurking in the codebase.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Audit all test files for trivial assertions that are always true/false
- [ ] Audit for assertions that don't actually verify behavior (just check non-empty)
- [ ] Replace trivial assertions with meaningful ones
- [ ] Add proper style/content verification where output is captured

## Patterns to find
- assert!(x || true)
- assert!(x || !x) 
- assert!(!x.is_empty()) without checking content
- let _ = something; // Just checking it doesn't crash

## Files to audit
- tests/conformance/*.rs
- src/**/*.rs (inline tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:49.161430662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867626646Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867605776Z","close_reason":"Replaced trivial assertions with meaningful content/width checks","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
